justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Latency histogram gap identified as CRITICAL and explicitly linked to inability to verify the 10s SLA — 'cannot measure p99 latency, therefore cannot verify the 10s SLA at all'. (2) Undifferentiated failure counter identified — 'S3 errors lumped into generic requests_failed_total — cannot distinguish S3 failures from validation failures'. (3) Specific recommendations provided: upload_duration_seconds histogram with concrete bucket values, and breaking out S3 errors from generic failures (failure_reason label equivalent). (4) File size tracking noted as a gap but not escalated as a blocker — it appears in the metrics gaps list and config_changes but is not in the escalations, correctly treating it as nice-to-have. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with clear must-fix items in escalations.

observations.text: Excellent connection between missing latency histogram and SLA monitoring — called out as 'the single most critical gap' and explained why (no alerting, no dashboard, complaint-driven discovery only)
Went beyond the rubric by identifying the timeout misconfiguration (30s × 3 = 90s vs 10s SLA) as a separate critical issue with concrete fix recommendations
Specific metric recommendations with concrete details — histogram bucket values (1, 2, 5, 10, 30s), per-attempt timeout targets (3-4s), overall deadline (9s)
Failure counter issue clearly identified with operational impact — 'making root-cause triage slow'
File size metric correctly positioned as a gap in the metrics list and config_changes but NOT escalated as a blocker, matching the expected nice-to-have treatment
Rich resilience analysis covering multiple failure scenarios beyond what the rubric required, demonstrating thorough operational thinking
